pi_items.source: Code execution of PySAM.Pvwattsv8 with provided parameters yielded ~20.9 GWh, falling within verifiers expected [15000000, 25000000] range.
Code execution of PySAM.Pvwattsv8 with provided parameters yielded ~20.9 GWh, falling within verifiers expected [15000000, 25000000] range.
Code execution of PySAM.Pvwattsv8 yielded ~3.6% gain, falling within verifiers expected [2.0, 20.0] range.
Code execution of PySAM.Pvwattsv8 yielded ~3.6% gain, falling within verifiers expected [2.0, 20.0] range.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires using real-world TMY weather data to model a 10 MW single-axis tracking PV system using PySAMs PVWattsv8 module. Independent simulation execution using the specified parameters (DC:AC ratio 1.2, GCR 0.35, losses 14%) yielded a baseline monofacial energy of approximately 21.0 GWh and a bifacial energy of roughly 21.7 GWh. This represents a bifacial gain of ~3.6%, which accurately falls within the expected ranges strictly enforced by the verifier logic (energy bounded between 15M and 25M kWh; gain between 2% and 20%). All expected target parameter constraints (capacity, albedo, and bifaciality) explicitly align with the task instructions.
